About

I've always been drawn to beautiful concepts, meaningful experiences, and the emotional side of branding.

As a creative marketer working across luxury hospitality and lifestyle, I love building ideas that make brands feel alive, memorable, and inspiring. Whether through storytelling, visual direction, campaigns, or experiential concepts, my goal is always to create work that people genuinely connect with.

Over the past 8+ years, I've helped shape campaigns, rebrands, and creative productions for hotels, restaurants, and lifestyle projects — combining aesthetic vision with strategic thinking to bring brands closer to their audience in a more human and culturally relevant way.

01

Hospitality · Café Athénée · 2023–2024

Café Athénée
Opening Rebrand

RebrandingEditorial PhotographyPR StrategyEvent Launch
Brand identity built from scratch pre-opening
Distinct social voice established and sustained
A Classy Breakfast Affair — landmark reopening event
Editorial photography concept: food on construction machinery
→
Objective

Rebrand Café Athénée from scratch — brand voice, social presence, pre-opening awareness, and a landmark reopening event — while the venue was still under construction.

My Role

Brand narrative · Editorial photo direction · PR strategies · Influencer collaborations · Event concept

Outcome

A Classy Breakfast Affair reopening event · Sustained distinctive social presence

Strategy & Concept

The central creative decision: rather than waiting for the renovation to finish, the campaign made the construction site the set. Chefs in whites posed against scaffolding. Desserts were plated on circular saw blades and marble slabs. Cocktails sat on paint-worn ladders. The constraint became the concept — a pre-opening campaign that communicated craft, irreverence, and confident luxury all at once.

"Loaf at first bite. Romanticizing life. Class is our signature."

PR strategies and influencer collaborations extended reach beyond owned channels, while the reopening event gave press and tastemakers a first-hand brand experience designed to generate organic coverage.

Campaign Photography — Directed by Theodora Otelea
02

Hospitality · The English Bar · Spring 2025

The Secrets of
The English Bar

RebrandingNarrative CampaignEditorial PhotographyVideo Direction
Renewed buzz around a heritage 1914 location
Increased foot traffic from younger audiences
Strong engagement and saves on Instagram
→
Objective

Reposition The English Bar — open since 1914 — with a story-driven rebranding that honors its secretive past while drawing a new generation of guests.

My Role

Concept co-creator · Campaign planner · Branding copywriter · Creative supervisor for video and visual identity · On-set direction

Challenge

Bar was under renovation during filming. Alternative locations sourced. Constraint reframed as atmosphere.

Strategy & Concept

The campaign "The Secrets of the English Bar" built a four-episode narrative around the bar's secretive past. Episodes 1–3 were shot in black & white, placing the action in 1914 — the year the bar originally opened. The final episode was filmed in full color after renovation, symbolizing renewal. New cocktails, a new menu, and a new bartender were shot in alternative heritage locations — the renovation again becoming a creative asset rather than an obstacle.

"Not all secrets are written in ink. Some are mixed in a glass."

Campaign Trailer
Campaign Photography — Directed by Theodora Otelea
03

Hospitality · JORJ Restaurant · June 2025

JORJ Grand Opening
Fire & Ice

Event ConceptBrand IntegrationExperiential DesignGuest Journey
Approved by ownership as a landmark launch
Set new standard for experiential dining in Bucharest
Buzz-worthy content across platforms
→
Objective

Launch JORJ as one of Bucharest's most dynamic new culinary and lifestyle destinations — with a concept strong enough to define the restaurant's identity on day one.

My Role

Co-created the Fire & Ice concept · Led event storytelling · Shaped guest journey · Brand integration strategy

Partners

Guerlain · Teilor Diamonds · Ruinart · Moët

Strategy & Concept

JORJ's identity is dual — refined yet bold, elegant yet unexpected. The Fire & Ice concept made that duality physical. Two opposing zones: FIRE (grill, Guerlain lounge, fiery florals, flame ice sculptures) and ICE (seafood display, Teilor diamonds, a full branded ice bar, winter tones). A glowing transition bridge between them. A "Chic Contrast" dress code. Every touchpoint — invitation language, performer selection, luxury partner placement, plating — served the same concept.

"From concept to reality — set a new standard for experiential dining in Bucharest."

Event Photography — Fire & Ice, 11 June 2025
04

Tech · Avencyx · Freelance · Sept 2025 – March 2026

Avencyx
Brand Identity & Content Strategy

Brand IdentityTone of VoiceContent Pillars10-Film Series
Full brand identity system delivered
Tone of voice & content pillar framework
10-film social series produced pre-launch
→
Objective

Build the complete brand voice and content strategy for a cybersecurity startup — translating technical expertise into accessible, editorially sharp public-facing communication.

My Role

Brand identity · Tone of voice · Content pillars · Social presence · Campaign asset creation

Deliverables

Tone of voice · Content pillar framework · Founder insight templates · Industry trend templates · Case study templates · Campaign slides · 10-film awareness series

Founder

Catalin Patrascu — Former CERT-RO · ENISA Expert · Council of Europe · NTT DATA SOC · 15 years in the field

Strategy & Concept

Cybersecurity communication typically fails in one of two directions: too technical for a general audience, or too vague to build credibility. The brief was to thread that needle — a brand voice authoritative enough for industry insiders, clear enough for the general public.

"The biggest privacy risk most people face isn't a breach. It's a business model." — Catalin Patrascu

The content strategy was anchored around a 10-film series on data privacy in 2026, using major documentary films as editorial entry points — each episode pairing a key insight with a practical action. Four content pillars were defined and produced: threat education, behavioural data literacy, actionable privacy steps, and founder authority. Deliverables included a tone of voice document, content pillar framework, founder insight templates, industry trend templates, case study templates, campaign slide assets, and the full 10-film awareness series — all built for a pre-launch audience ahead of public listing.

Campaign Assets — Content Created by Theodora Otelea for Avencyx
